source.title: De novo design of luciferases using deep learning

initial_request.input_data: 4,000 native small-molecule-binding proteins (for initial docking), 85 experimental structures of NTF2-like proteins (for extracting residue-residue distance and orientation distributions), and synthetic luciferin substrates (diphenylterazine/DTZ and 2-deoxycoelenterazine).
initial_request.user_intent: To overcome the limitations of native luciferases (such as misfolding, low substrate specificity, and cofactor requirements) by designing de novo luciferases from scratch that are small, highly stable, well-expressed in cells, and highly specific to synthetic luciferins for use as molecular probes in bioassays and multiplexed imaging.
initial_request.quantifiable_goal: A small molecular weight (~13.9 kDa), high thermostability (melting temperature > 95 °C), and a high catalytic efficiency (kcat/Km = 10^6 M^-1 s^-1) on diphenylterazine, comparable to native luciferases but with much higher substrate specificity.

evidence.support: 摘要说明了针对合成 luciferin 底物设计人工荧光素酶的目标。

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Native NTF2 structures have a range of pocket sizes and shapes but contain non-ideal features like long loops that compromise stability. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Large numbers of ideal NTF2-like structures with suitable pockets for DTZ binding. [Decision] To create large numbers of ideal NTF2-like structures, chose trRosetta to run Monte Carlo searches in sequence space and structure prediction on 2,000 naturally occurring NTF2s, expecting an essentially unlimited number of hallucinated NTF2 scaffolds with shape-complementary binding pockets.

parameters.search_method: Monte Carlo searches in sequence space
parameters.loss_function: confidence of the neural network in the predicted structure supplemented with a topology-specific loss function over core residue pair geometries
parameters.loop_regions: allowed the number of residues to vary

agent_trajectory.observation: Generated 1,615 family-wide hallucinated NTF2 scaffolds that provided more shape-complementary binding pockets for DTZ than native small-molecule-binding proteins.

evidence.support: 论文描述了基于 trRosetta、以天然 NTF2 序列为起点的 Monte Carlo 序列搜索。
论文报告生成了 1,615 个 family-wide hallucinated NTF2 scaffold。

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: 1,615 family-wide hallucinated NTF2 scaffolds have been generated. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Placement of catalytic residues to stabilize the anionic state of DTZ and lower the single-electron transfer (SET) energy barrier. [Decision] To design a shape-complementary catalytic site, chose RifGen and RifDock to enumerate rotamer interaction fields (RIFs) and dock anionic DTZ conformers against hallucinated NTF2 scaffolds, expecting scaffolds with optimized side chain–DTZ interactions including a key arginine residue.

parameters.RIFs_grid: three-dimensional grids consisting of millions of placements of amino acid side chains making hydrogen-bonding and nonpolar interactions
parameters.key_catalytic_residue: arginine guanidinium group placed adjacent to the N1 atom of the imidazopyrazinone group
parameters.selection_cutoff: top 50,000 docks with the most favourable side chain-DTZ interactions

agent_trajectory.observation: An average of eight side-chain rotamers, including an arginine residue to stabilize the anionic imidazopyrazinone core, were positioned in each pocket. The top 50,000 docks were selected for sequence optimization.

evidence.locator: De novo design of luciferases for DTZ
evidence.support: 论文描述了 DTZ 构象生成、RifGen RIF 枚举和 RifDock 放置流程。

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Top 50,000 docks with favorable side chain-DTZ interactions are identified. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: The remainder of the sequence needs optimization for high-affinity binding and foldability while maintaining the catalytic geometry and structural specificity. [Decision] To optimize the sequence for high-affinity binding to DTZ, chose RosettaDesign to run two-step sequence optimization and filtering on the top 50,000 docks, expecting a set of highly stable and specific designs ready for experimental screening.

parameters.step_1_constraints: identities of all RIF and HBNet residues kept fixed, surrounding residues optimized
parameters.step_2_constraints: RIF residue identities (except arginine) allowed to vary
parameters.relaxation: scaffold backbone, side chains and DTZ substrate allowed to relax in Cartesian space
parameters.filtering_criteria: ligand-binding energy, protein-ligand hydrogen bonds, shape complementarity and contact molecular surface

agent_trajectory.observation: 7,648 designs were selected and ordered as pooled oligos for experimental screening.

evidence.support: 论文报告经过序列优化后选出 7,648 个设计用于实验筛选。

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: 7,648 computational designs for DTZ luciferases have been generated as pooled oligos. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Experimental validation to identify which computational designs actually possess luciferase activity in vitro and in vivo. [Decision] To identify active luciferases, chose E. coli expression and colony-based screening to perform in vivo expression and luminescence imaging on the 7,648 designs, expecting clones with confirmed luciferase activity.

tool.name: E. coli expression and colony-based screening

parameters.screening_method: colony-based screening and 96-well plate expression confirmation

agent_trajectory.observation: Three active designs were identified. The most active, LuxSit, is 117 residues (13.9 kDa), highly expressed, soluble, monomeric, and not fully unfolded at 95 °C, with an emission peak around 480 nm.

evidence.support: 论文描述了大肠杆菌表达、菌落成像和 96 孔板验证流程。

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: LuxSit was successfully designed for DTZ, establishing a working pipeline for de novo luciferases. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Luciferases specific to another substrate, h-CTZ, which has a different molecular shape from DTZ. [Decision] To create h-CTZ-specific luciferases, chose AlphaFold2 and ProteinMPNN to perform pocket matching, catalytic site installation, and sequence design on additional NTF2 superfamily scaffolds, expecting active luciferases specific for the h-CTZ substrate.

parameters.scaffolds: additional set of NTF2 superfamily scaffolds with matching pocket shapes
parameters.catalytic_geometry: histidine and arginine substrate interaction geometries successful in the first round
parameters.filtering_criteria: AlphaFold2-predicted pLDDT, Cα RMSD, contact molecular surface and Rosetta-computed binding energies

agent_trajectory.observation: Identified 2 designs (HTZ3-D2 and HTZ3-G4) out of 46 that had luciferase activity with h-CTZ. Both were highly soluble, monodisperse, and monomeric.

evidence.locator: De novo design of luciferases for h-CTZ
evidence.support: 论文描述了利用第一轮设计经验创建 h-CTZ 特异性荧光素酶。

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: LuxSit has confirmed activity but its catalytic efficiency could potentially be improved. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Understanding of the specific contributions of active-site residues to catalysis and further optimization of the enzyme's turnover rate. [Decision] To optimize luciferase activity and understand catalysis, chose site-saturation mutagenesis (SSM) to systematically mutate and screen substrate-binding pocket residues in LuxSit, expecting variants with higher photon flux and catalytic efficiency.

parameters.target_residues: each residue in the substrate-binding pocket
parameters.mutation_strategy: mutated to every other amino acid one at a time

agent_trajectory.observation: Identified A96M and M110V mutants which increased activity by 16-fold and 19-fold. Guided by this, LuxSit-i (R60S/A96L/M110V) was created, achieving a >100-fold higher photon flux and a catalytic efficiency (kcat/Km) of 10^6 M^-1 s^-1.

evidence.support: 论文描述了针对底物结合口袋残基的位点饱和突变。
论文报告 LuxSit-i 的 photon flux 比 LuxSit 提高超过 100 倍。

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: LuxSit-i is a highly efficient and specific de novo luciferase in vitro. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Validation of its utility as a genetic tag and reporter in live mammalian cells, and demonstration of its multiplexing capability due to high substrate specificity. [Decision] To evaluate expression, function, and multiplexing capability, chose mammalian cell culture and luminescence imaging to perform transient expression and substrate/spectral-resolved assays in HEK293T cells expressing LuxSit-i and RLuc, expecting proof of high specificity and simultaneous pathway monitoring in live cells.

tool.name: Mammalian cell culture and luminescence imaging

parameters.activators: human tumour necrosis factor (TNF) and forskolin (FSK)

agent_trajectory.observation: LuxSit-i showed exquisite specificity (50-fold selectivity for DTZ over bis-CTZ, >100-fold over other luciferins). Successfully demonstrated simultaneous multiplexed reporting of NF-κB and cAMP-PKA signaling pathways in the same sample.

evidence.support: 论文描述了 HEK293T 活细胞表达和 DTZ 特异性发光验证。
论文描述了基于 HEK293T 细胞的多路 reporter assay。

success_verification.validation_technique: In vitro biochemical characterization (SDS-PAGE, SEC, CD spectroscopy, luminescence kinetics) and in vivo mammalian cell assays (HEK293T live-cell imaging, multiplexed signaling pathway reporting).

Molecular Weight.interpretation: LuxSit is 117 residues, smaller than any previously described luciferase, meeting the goal of a small molecular weight.

Thermostability.interpretation: CD melting experiments showed the protein is not fully unfolded at 95 °C and regains full structure upon cooling, meeting the high thermostability goal.

Catalytic Efficiency (kcat/Km).interpretation: LuxSit-i achieved a catalytic efficiency of 10^6 M^-1 s^-1 on DTZ, comparable to native luciferases, meeting the quantifiable goal.

Substrate Specificity.interpretation: LuxSit-i exhibited 50-fold selectivity for DTZ over bis-CTZ and >100-fold over other luciferin substrates, far exceeding the specificity of native luciferases.

success_verification.final_verdict: The study successfully achieved the de novo design of artificial luciferases (LuxSit and its optimized variant LuxSit-i) from scratch. The designed enzymes perfectly met all quantifiable goals: they are extremely small (~13.9 kDa), highly thermostable (Tm > 95 °C), possess high catalytic efficiency (10^6 M^-1 s^-1) on the synthetic substrate DTZ, and exhibit exquisite substrate specificity that enables multiplexed bioassays in mammalian cells.

evidence.support: 论文报告了催化效率、底物选择性和活细胞验证结果。
论文说明了源数据、设计模型和质粒等材料的可获得性。

quality.notes: 种子 case 来自赛事提供的样例文件，并补充了页面级证据指针。

source.title: Machine learning-guided co-optimization of fitness and diversity facilitates combinatorial library design in enzyme engineering

initial_request.target_name: Generalist Rma cytochrome c biocatalysts for C-B and C-Si bond formation
initial_request.input_data: Crystal structure of Rma cytochrome c (PDB 6CUK), six targeted active-site/front-loop residues (75, 99, 100, 101, 102, 103), prior directed-evolution knowledge for borylation and silylation, and MODIFY Pareto library design algorithm.
initial_request.user_intent: Engineer cytochrome c variants that can catalyze both carbon-boron and carbon-silicon bond formation with strong activity and stereocontrol, while exploring a broader and more diverse sequence space than standard saturation libraries.
initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Produce a combinatorial library whose screened variants outperform an NNK control in both reactions, including at least about 2x higher average yield and improved enantiomeric ratio, while uncovering novel high-performing generalist variants.

evidence.support: The paper states the aim of engineering cytochrome c variants that catalyze both C-B and C-Si bond formation with excellent efficiency and stereocontrol.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Existing Rma cytochrome c variants can catalyze new-to-nature carbene-transfer reactions, but the best evolved enzymes are reaction-specific. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: A compact library that targets the most relevant positions while preserving enough diversity to discover generalist catalysts. [Decision] Use the Rma cyt c crystal structure to define six targeted residues around the active site and front loop for combinatorial optimization, expecting these hotspots to control both activity and stereoselectivity.
agent_trajectory.observation: The study fixed on six active-site/front-loop residues as the designable positions for library construction.

evidence.support: The figure caption identifies residues 75, 99, 100, 101, 102, and 103 as the six targeted residues used to create the combinatorial libraries.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Six hotspots were selected for diversification. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Standard degenerate libraries either waste budget on nonfunctional variants or collapse onto a narrow region of sequence space. [Decision] Apply the MODIFY Pareto optimization algorithm, with optional residue-level priors, to co-optimize predicted fitness and sequence diversity when constructing the starting library.
agent_trajectory.observation: MODIFY generated a Pareto-style library design intended to populate multiple high-fitness regions rather than a single narrow optimum.

evidence.support: The paper describes MODIFY as balancing fitness and diversity and allowing residue-level diversity control with prior knowledge.
The methods section states that MODIFY designs combinatorial libraries with optimized sequence fitness and diversity over selected residues.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: A MODIFY-informed library has been designed. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether this library actually yields better catalysts than a standard NNK control in both reaction classes. [Decision] Randomly sample and screen 160 clones from the MODIFY library in both C-B and C-Si bond-forming reactions, quantifying yield and enantiomeric ratio by chiral HPLC.
agent_trajectory.observation: Across both assays, the MODIFY library outperformed the NNK control, indicating that the designed library was enriched for productive catalysts.

evidence.support: The paper reports random selection of 160 clones, dual screening in C-B and C-Si reactions, and chiral HPLC measurement of yield and enantiomeric ratio.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: MODIFY screening enriched for productive variants. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which variants best combine novelty, catalytic activity, and stereocontrol across both transformations. [Decision] Rank the screened variants by reaction performance and compare them to previously evolved RDT and TDE baselines, expecting novel hits rather than simple rediscovery of known solutions.
agent_trajectory.observation: The library yielded strong C-B and C-Si catalysts, including novel variants six mutations away from the previously evolved RDT and TDE enzymes; MLYPPT emerged as a particularly important generalist hit.

evidence.support: The paper states that the best-performing borylation and silylation variants are six mutations away from previously evolved RDT and TDE variants.
The paper states that MODIFY produced the generalist MLYPPT variant with C-B and C-Si performance superior or comparable to experimentally evolved enzymes.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Novel high-performing variants have been identified, including double-proline front-loop mutants. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Why these variants can support two different carbene-transfer reactions. [Decision] Run molecular dynamics analysis on top MODIFY variants to test whether front-loop conformational changes and flexibility explain reaction generality.
agent_trajectory.observation: MD showed substantial front-loop conformational and dynamic changes, supporting the idea that increased loop flexibility helps generalist variants accommodate both substrates.

evidence.support: The paper reports significant front-loop conformational changes in MODIFY variants and links improved loop flexibility of MLYPPT to generality across both reactions.
The paper identifies functional double-proline mutants including MLYPPT and SPIPAM with excellent catalytic activity and enantioselectivity.

success_verification.validation_technique: Whole-cell and cell-free biocatalysis assays for C-B and C-Si bond formation, followed by chiral HPLC quantification of yield and enantiomeric ratio, plus MD-based structural interpretation of top hits.

C-B average yield improvement.interpretation: The MODIFY library delivered a 2.2-fold higher average yield than the NNK control in carbon-boron bond formation.

C-B average enantiomeric ratio improveme.interpretation: The MODIFY library improved average enantiomeric ratio fourfold in carbon-boron bond formation.

C-Si average yield improvement.interpretation: The MODIFY library delivered a 1.9-fold higher average yield than the NNK control in carbon-silicon bond formation.

C-Si average enantiomeric ratio improvem.interpretation: The MODIFY library improved average enantiomeric ratio 1.3-fold in carbon-silicon bond formation.

success_verification.final_verdict: The study successfully used MODIFY to design a structurally informed, diversity-aware combinatorial library that produced novel and generalist Rma cytochrome c variants for both C-B and C-Si bond formation, outperforming a standard NNK library on key yield and stereocontrol metrics.

evidence.support: The paper reports 2.2-fold and 1.9-fold average yield gains and improved enantiomeric ratios relative to NNK for C-B and C-Si reactions.
The paper concludes that MODIFY enabled a generalist MLYPPT variant with performance superior or comparable to experimentally evolved biocatalysts.

quality.notes: 基于 Nature Communications 的 CC-BY 开放论文人工整理，并保留页面级证据指针。 人工扩展来源文件：data/curated/cases/SELT-PROT-0002.json

source.title: Ensemble-based enzyme design can recapitulate the effects of laboratory directed evolution in silico

initial_request.target_name: HG4 Kemp eliminase redesigned from an HG3/HG3.17 evolutionary trajectory
initial_request.input_data: HG-series Kemp eliminase lineage, room-temperature crystal structures with and without 6NT, HG3.17 mutation set near the active site, and computational enzyme-design models using fixed backbones or crystallographic ensemble refinement.
initial_request.user_intent: Understand which conformational changes produced the activity gains during laboratory evolution and use that knowledge to design a high-activity enzyme computationally, reducing the need for repeated wet-lab evolution.
initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Recover a Kemp eliminase with catalytic efficiency around 10^5 M^-1 s^-1, more than 700-fold above HG3 and close to HG3.17, while recapitulating the productive transition-state geometry using computational modeling.

evidence.support: The paper describes building HG4 from HG3 by introducing HG3.17 active-site and second-shell mutations to test whether computational design can approach the evolved activity.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: HG3.17 reached much higher catalytic efficiency than the original HG3 design after many directed-evolution mutations. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which mechanistic changes, rather than just sequence differences, explain the activity increase. [Decision] Audit the evolved trajectory and compare kinetics across HG-series variants to define the structural questions that need to be answered.
agent_trajectory.observation: The study established that HG3.17 was approximately three orders of magnitude better than HG3, motivating a structural analysis of the evolutionary path.

evidence.support: The paper states that mutations introduced into HG3 to produce HG3.17 increased catalytic efficiency by approximately three orders of magnitude.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Kinetic gains are established, but cryogenic structures may hide relevant conformational heterogeneity. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: How the accessible conformational ensemble changed during evolution. [Decision] Solve room-temperature crystal structures for all HG-series enzymes with and without bound 6NT to expose catalytically relevant heterogeneity.
agent_trajectory.observation: Room-temperature structures across the HG lineage revealed the bound pose and the conformational heterogeneity needed to analyze productive versus nonproductive substates.

evidence.support: The paper reports solving room-temperature structures of all HG-series Kemp eliminases in both ligand-bound and unbound states at 1.35-1.99 A resolution.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Structures across the trajectory are available. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which physical changes in the ensemble enabled higher catalysis. [Decision] Quantify rigidity, pre-organization, and active-site accessibility across the trajectory to identify the mutations and conformations that matter most.
agent_trajectory.observation: The analysis showed progressive rigidification near residue 50, widened access channels, and about 40% productive pre-organization in unbound HG3.17, providing a structural basis for the evolved activity gains.

evidence.support: The paper reports that HG3.17 has a large fraction of molecules pre-organized for catalysis and discusses loop rigidification and widened substrate access during evolution.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: The structural determinants of improved catalysis are clearer. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether these insights can be turned into a compact computational redesign. [Decision] Build HG4 by introducing eight first- and second-shell mutations from HG3.17 into HG3, focusing on positions near the transition state and at the channel entrance.
agent_trajectory.observation: HG4 was proposed as an eight-mutation redesign intended to capture the main catalytic improvements of HG3.17 without the full distal mutation set.

evidence.support: The paper states that HG4 includes HG3.17 mutations within 7.5 A of 6NT plus W275A and R276F to widen the active-site entrance.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: HG4 sequence has been defined. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Single fixed-backbone design may miss subtle but crucial conformational differences. [Decision] Compare HG4 modeling on the original 1GOR backbone, on HG3 crystal templates, and on HG3-derived ensemble-refinement templates to test which representation can recover the correct transition-state pose and energy.
agent_trajectory.observation: The 1GOR fixed backbone produced a model that differed substantially from the HG4 crystal structure and was destabilized by about 45 kcal/mol, while HG3-derived ensemble members recovered the correct transition-state binding mode with energies comparable to the HG4 crystal structure.

evidence.support: The paper reports that the 1GOR-derived model was destabilized by about 45 kcal/mol and that HG3-derived ensemble refinement recapitulated the correct binding mode with comparable energies.
The paper concludes that crystallographically derived HG3 ensembles can sample catalytically competent substates during design and may obviate additional directed evolution.

success_verification.validation_technique: Kinetic measurement of HG-series variants, room-temperature crystallography with and without 6NT, and computational comparison of fixed-backbone versus ensemble-refined design templates.

HG4 catalytic efficiency.interpretation: HG4 reached a catalytic efficiency of 103,000 M^-1 s^-1, placing it near the natural-enzyme range and close to the evolved HG3.17 benchmark.

Improvement over HG3.interpretation: HG4 was reported to be more than 700-fold more efficient than HG3.

Pre-organized HG3.17 molecules.interpretation: Approximately 40% of unbound HG3.17 molecules were already pre-organized for catalysis, explaining much of the evolutionary gain.

1GOR model destabilization.interpretation: Replacing the HG4 backbone context with the original 1GOR design template generated a substantially destabilized model, highlighting the need for ensemble-aware templates.

success_verification.final_verdict: The study successfully showed that ensemble-based computational design can capture key benefits of laboratory evolution: HG4 retained near-HG3.17 activity with only eight selected mutations, and the decisive modeling improvement came from using experimentally grounded backbone ensembles rather than a single fixed template.

evidence.support: The paper reports HG4 at 103,000 M^-1 s^-1 and HG3.17 at 126,000 M^-1 s^-1.
The paper states that HG4 is more than 700-fold better than HG3, equivalent to the average natural enzyme range, and only about 20% less active than HG3.17.

quality.notes: 基于 Nature Communications 的 CC-BY 开放论文人工整理，并保留页面级证据指针。 人工扩展来源文件：data/curated/cases/SELT-PROT-0003.json

source.title: AI-enabled Alkaline-resistant Evolution of Protein to Apply in Mass Production

initial_request.target_name: Alkali-resistant VHH antibody ligand for industrial growth-hormone purification
initial_request.input_data: A VHH antibody sequence binding growth hormone, Pro-PRIME protein language model scores, single-point mutant alkali-resistance and melting-temperature measurements, and industrial affinity-chromatography cleaning requirements using strong alkaline treatment.
initial_request.user_intent: Use an AI-guided protein engineering loop to evolve an antibody ligand that keeps growth-hormone binding after harsh alkaline cleaning, while also preserving or improving thermal stability and affinity for production use.
initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Complete a two-round design campaign with only dozens of experimentally tested mutants, identify multi-point variants with stronger alkali resistance and thermal stability than wild type and best single mutants, and validate that the selected ligand remains usable across repeated industrial purification cycles.

evidence.support: The paper describes using Pro-PRIME to improve alkali resistance, thermal stability, and affinity of a growth-hormone-binding VHH antibody through two rounds of design.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Affinity chromatography ligands for growth hormone face alkaline cleaning conditions that most natural proteins do not tolerate well. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether a language model trained mostly on natural protein sequences can design resistance to such rare, unnatural industrial stress. [Decision] Frame alkali resistance as the primary target while tracking thermal stability and affinity as coupled constraints, because a production ligand must survive cleaning without losing binding function.
agent_trajectory.observation: The engineering objective was converted into a multi-property fitness problem: retain target binding after high-pH exposure while keeping the ligand stable enough for repeated industrial cycles.

evidence.support: The assessment states that Pro-PRIME was applied to engineer VHH antibodies with enhanced stability for extreme industrial environments.
The paper explains that industrial proteins often need to work under extreme pH or temperature conditions that are scarce in natural training data.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: The target VHH sequence and industrial fitness objectives are defined, but no task-specific alkali-resistance training data are available yet. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which single amino-acid changes are worth spending wet-lab budget on. [Decision] Use Pro-PRIME in a zero-shot setting to score saturated single-point mutants and select the top 45 candidates for experimental testing, accepting that this first round mainly builds a compact empirical map.

parameters.design_space: saturated single-point mutations of the VHH antibody

agent_trajectory.observation: Pro-PRIME produced a ranked single-mutant shortlist, allowing the team to test only 45 candidates rather than launching a broad random or rational-design screen.

evidence.support: The paper states that Pro-PRIME scored single-site saturated mutants and that the top 45 VHH single-point mutants were selected for testing.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Forty-five single mutants have been selected by zero-shot language-model scoring. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which mutations actually improve alkali resistance, melting temperature, and affinity under assay conditions. [Decision] Express and test the 45 variants after alkaline treatment, measuring EC50-linked affinity and melting temperature so the first round can both identify useful hits and provide training data for the harder combinatorial round.

tool.name: Alkali exposure, affinity assay, and melting-temperature measurement

parameters.alkali_treatment: 0.3 M NaOH for 24 h; selected variants also tested at 0.5 M NaOH

agent_trajectory.observation: Fifteen single-point mutants improved alkali resistance, 35 improved melting temperature, and six mutants improved all three tracked properties, creating a high-value empirical set for round-two model tuning.

evidence.support: The paper reports that 15 of 45 single mutants showed higher alkali resistance, 35 showed higher Tm, and six improved alkali resistance, thermal stability, and affinity.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Round one produced measured single-mutant fitness data, but multi-point combinations introduce epistasis and a much larger search space. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which combinations escape local optima while maintaining thermal stability. [Decision] Fine-tune Pro-PRIME separately on alkali-resistance and Tm data from the single-mutant campaign, then prioritize multi-point variants with high alkali-resistance scores and non-decreased Tm scores.

parameters.selection_rule: high alkali-resistance score with Tm score no lower than wild type

agent_trajectory.observation: Fine-tuned Pro-PRIME selected 20 multi-point mutants, and five surpassed the best single-point mutant in alkali resistance while multi-point Tm values were generally higher.

evidence.support: The paper states that two Pro-PRIME models were trained with single-mutant alkali-resistance and Tm data, 20 multi-point mutants were selected, and five exceeded the best single-point mutant for alkali resistance.
The paper reports that zero-shot multi-point prediction had a Spearman correlation of -0.71 with experimental results, highlighting the value of fine-tuning.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Several multi-point variants outperform single-mutant baselines, but simple additive reasoning cannot explain the best combinations. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether the model found non-obvious epistatic interactions rather than merely stacking individually positive mutations. [Decision] Compare single- and multi-mutant effects, then use structural and molecular-dynamics analysis on representative variants to interpret how distant mutation pairs improve resistance.

tool.name: Epistasis analysis with molecular dynamics interpretation

agent_trajectory.observation: The study identified a double-negative-yields-positive pattern for A57D;P29T in alkali resistance and used MD to support a stability-based explanation involving increased rigidity and hydrogen bonding rather than a large fold change.

evidence.support: The paper reports that A57D;P29T showed high alkali resistance even though A57D and P29T were individually negative for that property.
The paper describes MD analysis of A57D;P29T, including lower RMSD, reduced residue fluctuations, and additional hydrogen bonds as a stability explanation.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: The best designed VHH mutants improve alkali resistance, Tm, and sometimes affinity in laboratory assays. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether these gains translate into reusable chromatography material under production cleaning cycles. [Decision] Evaluate residual dynamic binding capacity after 0.5 M NaOH exposure and monitor affinity-chromatography yield over repeated production cycles, because real industrial value depends on reuse stability rather than a single assay win.

tool.name: Dynamic binding capacity and chromatography-cycle validation

parameters.stress_condition: 0.5 M NaOH for 6 h and 24 h

agent_trajectory.observation: Designed multi-site mutants retained much more DBC after alkaline stress than wild type, and the selected mutant was used in practical growth-hormone production without a significant yield decline across many chromatography cycles.

evidence.support: The paper states that the design process can be completed within two months and that the mutants were applied in the purification process of growth hormones.
The paper reports 60-90% residual DBC after 6 h alkaline treatment for designed mutants and more than 50% residual DBC after 24 h for some variants, compared with 15.2% for wild type.

success_verification.validation_technique: Two-round computational and wet-lab validation: zero-shot Pro-PRIME single-mutant scoring, alkaline-treatment affinity assays, melting-temperature measurements, fine-tuned multi-point design, epistasis/MD analysis, and industrial dynamic-binding-capacity plus chromatography-cycle testing.

experimentally tested mutants.interpretation: The complete two-round campaign validated 45 single-point and 20 multi-point mutants, demonstrating a compact design-build-test loop.

single-mutant alkali-resistance hit rate.interpretation: One third of the first-round single-point variants showed improved alkali resistance.

best single-mutant alkali-resistance gai.interpretation: The best single-point mutant improved alkali resistance by about 44.7% relative to wild type.

best multi-point alkali-resistance gain.interpretation: The best multi-point mutant improved alkali resistance by about 67.7%, surpassing the best single-mutant result.

thermal-stability round-two success rate.interpretation: All round-two multi-point mutants improved thermal stability under the reported criterion.

best multi-point Tm gain.interpretation: The best multi-point variant increased melting temperature by about 10.02 C over wild type.

zero-shot multi-point Spearman correlati.interpretation: Poor zero-shot correlation for multi-point mutants justified fine-tuning on the first-round wet-lab data.

wild-type residual DBC after 24 h alkali.interpretation: Wild-type residual dynamic binding capacity after 24 h alkaline treatment was far below the designed multi-site mutants.

success_verification.final_verdict: The study is a strong Sci-Evo example because it records a full scientific evolution loop from industrial objective definition, through AI candidate generation and wet-lab feedback, to fine-tuned epistatic multi-point design and production validation. The decisive improvement came from using first-round experimental data to fine-tune Pro-PRIME for the multi-point search rather than relying on zero-shot predictions alone.

evidence.support: The paper reports 44.7% best single-mutant alkali-resistance improvement, 67.7% best multi-point improvement, 100% thermal-stability success in round two, and a 10.02 C best Tm increase.
The paper concludes that Pro-PRIME captured epistatic effects with limited data and that the designed VHH was deployed in practical production.

quality.notes: 基于 eLife / PMC 开放论文人工整理，保留页面级证据指针；本 case 是当前数据集中最强的 AI 闭环工业验证样例。 人工扩展来源文件：data/curated/cases/SELT-PROT-0004.json

source.title: Design of an improved universal signal peptide based on the α-factor mating secretion signal for enzyme production in yeast

initial_request.target_name: Universal alpha-factor preproleader for improved fungal enzyme secretion in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
initial_request.input_data: Native alpha-factor preproleader, evolved alpha9H2 leader mutations from prior laccase directed-evolution campaigns, PK2 and ApL laccase secretion assays, 96-well ABTS activity screens, and later validation across fungal oxidoreductases and hydrolases.
initial_request.user_intent: Infer which signal-peptide mutations genuinely improve enzyme secretion, separate beneficial mutations from neutral or deleterious baggage accumulated during directed evolution, and build a compact leader that works across multiple enzyme classes.
initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Design an optimized leader that matches or beats the evolved alpha9H2 benchmark for PK2 and ApL laccase secretion, generalizes to other fungal enzymes with multi-fold secretion gains, and yields a practical follow-up rule for target-specific optimization at positions 86 and 87.

evidence.support: The abstract states that the study optimized the alpha-factor preproleader by bottom-up and top-down strategies and identified synergistic mutations in the final alphaOPT leader.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: The alpha-factor preproleader is widely used for yeast secretion, and an evolved alpha9H2 leader improves laccase production. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: A universal leader must avoid merely inheriting mutations that helped one enzyme but hurt or do nothing for others. [Decision] Benchmark alpha9H2 against the native leader with two model laccases, then use the result as the upper reference for systematic redesign.

tool.name: Yeast secretion benchmark with ABTS laccase assay

agent_trajectory.observation: The evolved alpha9H2 leader produced roughly 2-fold higher PK2 activity and 12-fold higher ApL activity than the native leader, confirming it as a strong but potentially overfit benchmark.

evidence.support: The paper reports that alpha9H2 yielded about twofold higher PK2 activity and 12-fold higher ApL activity than the native leader in 96-well ABTS assays.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: alpha9H2 is better than the native leader, but its mutation set mixes potentially beneficial, neutral, and harmful substitutions. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which individual mutations drive secretion gains across enzymes. [Decision] Add or remove 13 candidate mutations one at a time in the native-leader context and test both PK2 and ApL, so mutation effects can be compared before building combinations.
agent_trajectory.observation: The single-mutation scan localized broadly useful effects near the pre-region, especially Aα9D and Aα20T, while revealing neutral or deleterious positions such as Qα32H, Fα48S, Sα58G, and Gα62R depending on enzyme context.

evidence.support: The paper states that 13 single-mutated leaders were fused to PK2 and ApL, screened in 96-well plates, and compared by normalized laccase activity with statistical testing.
The discussion identifies Aα9D and Aα20T as broadly beneficial mutations and notes that favorable effects were mostly located at or near the pre-region.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Several single mutations look beneficial, but secretion leaders are short regulatory peptides where mutation interactions can be strongly non-additive. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Which combinations preserve synergy instead of collapsing expression. [Decision] Build targeted double and quadruple combinations, then use in vivo recombination over selected leaders to search a larger combination space under a PK2 laccase screen.

tool.name: Targeted combinatorial mutagenesis and in vivo recombination

agent_trajectory.observation: The combinatorial screen exposed strong epistasis: some apparently good single mutations became detrimental together, while the best recombined clones converged on Aα9D and Aα9D,Aα20T leaders.

evidence.support: The paper describes targeted double and quadruple leader mutants, then screening 1600 recombined clones; the five fittest clones carried Aα9D or Aα9D,Aα20T leaders.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: Bottom-up results point to Aα9D and Aα20T, but alpha9H2 still contains multiple inherited mutations. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether some alpha9H2 mutations are passengers that reduce portability. [Decision] Start from alpha9H2 and revert suspected neutral or deleterious mutations to native residues, testing whether a simpler evolved leader keeps or improves secretion performance.

tool.name: Top-down reversion analysis of evolved leader

parameters.goal: remove neutral or deleterious accumulated mutations

agent_trajectory.observation: Top-down simplification agreed with the bottom-up pathway: the optimized signal could be reduced to the Aα9D,Aα20T-centered design while keeping the baseline Lα42S and Dα83E changes present in the working native leader.

evidence.support: The paper states that the top-down approach removed possible deleterious or neutral mutations from alpha9H2 that might mask beneficial effects.
The paper reports comparison of final bottom-up and top-down leaders and selection of the Aα9D,Aα20T design as alphaOPT.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: The simplified alphaOPT leader works on the two model laccases, but the central claim is broad enzyme secretion. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: Whether the leader generalizes beyond the training-like laccase pair. [Decision] Test alphaOPT across additional fungal oxidoreductases and hydrolases under microtiter and flask conditions, comparing it to native and alpha9H2 leaders.
agent_trajectory.observation: alphaOPT enhanced secretion for multiple fungal enzymes, often outperforming alpha9H2 and giving roughly 2- to 20-fold improvements over the native leader depending on the enzyme.

evidence.support: The paper states that alphaOPT was evaluated with additional fungal oxidoreductases and hydrolases to test broad secretion performance.
The paper reports that alphaOPT generally enhanced enzyme levels by roughly 2- to 20-fold over the native leader and outperformed alpha9H2 for most enzymes tested.

agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Known / completed: alphaOPT is a strong universal leader, but spacer residues 86 and 87 show enzyme-dependent effects. [Gap] Missing / unresolved: How to adapt the universal leader for a specific new target without restarting the full engineering campaign. [Decision] Propose and test combinatorial saturation mutagenesis at positions 86 and 87 in the alphaOPT context, using high-throughput secretion screening to tune the leader to the fused enzyme.

tool.name: Combinatorial saturation mutagenesis at alphaOPT positions 86 and 87

agent_trajectory.observation: Positions 86 and 87 behaved as target-specific secretion hotspots: their saturation libraries produced more diverse and useful activity landscapes than control N-glycosylation libraries, yielding a practical tuning rule for future enzymes.

evidence.support: The paper describes randomizing positions 86 and 87 in alphaOPT, comparing the libraries with N-glycosylation controls, and identifying these positions as hotspots for enzyme-specific secretion tuning.
The methods report CSM 86/87 libraries with all 20 standard amino-acid substitutions and screening of 1600 clones per CSM 86/87 library.

success_verification.validation_technique: Directed redesign was validated through 96-well yeast secretion assays, ABTS-based laccase activity readouts, targeted mutation and recombination screens, top-down evolved-leader reversion, cross-enzyme secretion testing, flask production, purification, deglycosylation, and target-specific CSM 86/87 screens.

alpha9H2 PK2 benchmark gain.interpretation: The evolved alpha9H2 leader gave roughly twofold higher PK2 secretion activity than the native leader.

alpha9H2 ApL benchmark gain.interpretation: The evolved alpha9H2 leader gave roughly 12-fold higher ApL secretion activity than the native leader.

single mutations tested.interpretation: The bottom-up scan tested 13 candidate leader mutations one at a time across two model laccases.

recombination screen size.interpretation: The in vivo recombination library screened 1600 PK2 clones and converged on Aα9D and Aα9D,Aα20T leaders.

alphaOPT broad secretion gain.interpretation: Across additional fungal enzymes, alphaOPT generally improved secretion over the native leader by roughly 2- to 20-fold.

CSM 86/87 screen size.interpretation: Target-specific optimization at positions 86 and 87 used 1600-clone saturation libraries to tune secretion for individual fused enzymes.

success_verification.final_verdict: The study is a useful Sci-Evo case because it reconstructs an engineering lineage rather than only reporting an endpoint: prior directed-evolution mutations are decomposed, tested singly and combinatorially, simplified from the evolved leader, validated across enzyme families, and converted into a reusable target-specific optimization rule.

evidence.support: The paper concludes that alphaOPT was obtained by a dual bottom-up and top-down design strategy and markedly enhances secretion of a range of fungal enzymes in yeast.
The article states that it is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.

quality.notes: 基于 Springer CC-BY 开放论文人工整理；该 case 补足非 AI 的 directed-evolution 轨迹拆解、组合突变和跨酶泛化验证。 人工扩展来源文件：data/curated/cases/SELT-PROT-0005.json
